---
title: "4.9 ArrayList Traversals"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Traversal patterns also apply to ArrayLists

`ArrayList` traversal can use:

::: {.tight-list}
- enhanced for loop
- indexed for loop
- while loop
:::

Choose the loop based on whether you need the index, whether the list changes, and whether you are reading objects.

## Enhanced For Loop

### Best for reading every element

```java
for (String name : names)
{
    System.out.println(name);
}
```

Use this when you do not need the index and do not need to add or remove while traversing.

## Indexed For Loop

### Use `get(i)` and `size()`

```java
for (int i = 0; i < names.size(); i++)
{
    System.out.println(names.get(i));
}
```

Common error: using `names.length` or `names[i]`, which are array syntax, not `ArrayList` syntax.

## While Loop

### Useful when index movement is conditional

```java
int i = 0;
while (i < names.size())
{
    System.out.println(names.get(i));
    i++;
}
```

`while` can be useful when the loop might skip, stop early, or remove elements with careful index control.

## Insert While Traversing

### Insertion changes later indices

```java
for (int i = 0; i < words.size(); i++)
{
    if (words.get(i).equals("target"))
    {
        words.add(i + 1, "found");
        i++;
    }
}
```

After insertion, increment carefully so the loop does not immediately process the new element by accident.

## ArrayList of Objects

### Use object methods during traversal

```java
for (Student s : students)
{
    if (s.getGpa() >= 3.5)
    {
        System.out.println(s.getName());
    }
}
```

The loop variable stores one object reference at a time.

## Groupwork Coding Challenge {.image-fit}

### FRQ Word Pairs

![Word pair construction pattern](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-9-arraylist-traversals/wordpairs.png){fig-align="center" width="45%"}

Build every pair of words with nested loops:

```java
for (int i = 0; i < words.length; i++)
{
    for (int j = i + 1; j < words.length; j++)
    {
        pairs.add(new WordPair(words[i], words[j]));
    }
}
```

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- traverse an `ArrayList` with enhanced for and indexed loops
- use `size()` and `get(i)` correctly
- explain why insertion or removal changes later indices
- traverse an `ArrayList` of objects using object methods
- use nested loops to create unique pairs
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
